Commands to Update: - jfp login (output: { authenticated: true, email, tier }) - jfp logout (output: { logged_out: true }) - jfp whoami (output: { email, tier, user_id }) - jfp collections list (output: { collections: [...] }) - jfp collections show (output: { collection, items: [...] }) - jfp notes (output: { prompt_id, notes: [...] }) - jfp save (output: { saved: true, prompt_id }) - jfp list --mine/--saved (output: { prompts: [...] }) Implementation: - Add --json/-j flag to all relevant commands - When --json is set, output only valid JSON to stdout - Errors should also be JSON: { error: true, message, code } - Progress/status messages go to stderr when --json is enabled - Ensure consistent field naming across all commands ","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-10T20:23:15.385023105-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T21:20:01.044458985-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T21:20:01.044458985-05:00","close_reason":"All CLI commands now have --json flag support with consistent output format","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-9r7s","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-cgj5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-10T20:25:08.868781233-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-9r7s","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-9g76","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-10T20:25:19.154441027-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} -{"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-9rgl","title":"Redesign 'Clear All Filters' as a proper button","description":"# Task: Redesign Clear Filters Button ## Current Implementation (page.tsx:165-172) ```tsx {hasActiveFilters && ( )} ``` ## Problems 1. Plain text link - low visual prominence 2. Uses indigo (inconsistent with neutral design system) 3. Only visible when filters active (no affordance) 4. No icon - relies entirely on text ## Proposed Design ### Option A: Always-visible ghost button (Recommended) ```tsx ``` ### Option B: Only when active, but as a proper button ```tsx {hasActiveFilters && ( )} ``` ## Implementation Details ### Add filter count calculation ```tsx const filterCount = useMemo(() => { let count = 0; if (filters.query) count++; if (filters.category) count++; count += filters.tags.length; return count; }, [filters]); ``` ### Add accessibility ```tsx aria-label={`Clear all ${filterCount} active filters`} ``` ## Design Rationale - Ghost button maintains visual hierarchy (filters are secondary to results) - Always visible = discoverable (disabled state indicates "nothing to clear") - Icon + text = clear meaning - Count badge = precise feedback - Matches CategoryFilter/TagFilter clear button pattern ## Testing 1. With no filters: button disabled, grayed out 2. With 1 filter: button enabled, shows "(1)" 3. With multiple: button shows count 4. Click clears all and disables button 5. Keyboard accessible (Tab, Enter) ## Files Modified - apps/web/src/app/page.tsx ## Dependencies - Should use the updated neutral color palette (do after color fix) - Matches pattern already used in TagFilter.tsx (Clear button)","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-12T00:38:18.479565228-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T02:31:16.391834415-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-9rgl","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-ogel","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-12T00:43:15.125413399-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} +{"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-9rgl","title":"Redesign 'Clear All Filters' as a proper button","description":"# Task: Redesign Clear Filters Button ## Current Implementation (page.tsx:165-172) ```tsx {hasActiveFilters && ( )} ``` ## Problems 1. With no filters: button disabled, grayed out 2. With 1 filter: button enabled, shows "(1)" 3. With multiple: button shows count 4. Click clears all and disables button 5. Configure CLI E2E tests to run on push/PR. Configure Playwright for web E2E with proper browser setup. Upload test artifacts (screenshots, videos, logs) on failure. Optional: multi-browser test matrix for comprehensive coverage.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-10T17:43:26.454174609-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T19:16:26.26586356-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T19:16:26.26586356-05:00","close_reason":"CI infrastructure implemented in parent EPIC","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-9sj","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-jxd","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-10T17:53:26.00812535-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-9sj","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-945","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-10T17:53:36.860791733-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-9sj","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-nfm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-10T17:53:59.405040418-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-9wk9","title":"EPIC: Ratings & Reviews System","description":"## Origin Moved from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-cdp. ## Overview Ratings and reviews provide quality signals for community content, helping users discover the best prompts, packs, and skills while rewarding creators for quality contributions. ## Rating System Design ### Simple Rating (MVP) - Thumbs up / thumbs down (binary) - Easy, low-friction interaction - Clear positive/negative signal - Calculate "approval rate" percentage ### Extended Rating (V2) - 1-5 star rating - Written review (optional) - Helpful/not helpful on reviews - Response from author ## Key Features ### Rate Content - One-click rating on any ratable content - Rate: prompts, packs, skills - Change rating anytime - See your rating history ### View Ratings - Show rating on content cards - Rating breakdown on detail pages - Sort by rating in lists - Filter by minimum rating ### Reviews (V2) - Write text review with rating - Edit/delete own reviews - Report inappropriate reviews - Author can respond to reviews ### Leaderboards - Top rated prompts this week/month - Top contributors by ratings received - Rising stars (new content with high ratings) ## Anti-Gaming Measures - One rating per user per item - Rate limiting on rapid ratings - Flag suspicious patterns - Weight by user reputation ## Database Schema - ratings (id, user_id, content_type, content_id, rating_value, created_at, updated_at) - reviews (id, user_id, content_type, content_id, rating_id, content, created_at) - review_votes (review_id, user_id, is_helpful) ## UI Components - RatingButton (thumbs up/down) - RatingDisplay (show current rating) - RatingBreakdown (detailed stats) - ReviewList (paginated reviews) - ReviewForm (write review) ## Integration Points - Community prompts (Swap Meet) - Prompt packs - Skills - Collections (V2) ","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-11T01:20:02.532432863-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T01:20:02.532432863-05:00"} {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-9wpw","title":"Fix useLocalStorage null persistence","description":"useLocalStorage skipped persisting null values because null used as sentinel; track latest value with a separate flag","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-10T17:55:53.211070932-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T17:56:21.167849624-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T17:56:21.167849624-05:00","close_reason":"Track latest value with flag so null can persist safely"} @@ -193,7 +193,7 @@ {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-ezv2","title":"Implement changelog and what's new feature","description":"## Origin Moved from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-xj4e. ## Task Implement a changelog to communicate new features and updates to users. ## Why This Matters - Users know about new features - Reduces "I didn't know you could do that" - Shows product is actively developed - Marketing opportunity ## Components ### 1. Changelog Page - /changelog - Public page - Chronological list of updates - Categories: New, Improved, Fixed - Date for each entry - SEO-friendly ### 2. What's New Modal - Shown to users after update - Highlights major changes - Dismissible (remember dismissal) - Link to full changelog ### 3. Update Badge - Small badge on nav item - "1 new update" indicator - Clears when viewed ### 4. Email Digest (optional) - Monthly changelog email - Major updates only - For engaged users ## Changelog Entry Format ```markdown ## March 2026 ### New - **Collections Sharing**: Share entire collections with a link - **Dark Mode**: Toggle between light and dark themes ### Improved - Search is now 2x faster - Better mobile navigation ### Fixed - Fixed issue with export failing for large collections - Corrected billing date display ``` ## Database ```typescript export const changelogEntries = pgTable("changelog_entries", { id: uuid("id").primaryKey().defaultRandom(), version: text("version"), // optional semver title: text("title").notNull(), content: text("content").notNull(), // markdown category: text("category").notNull(), // new, improved, fixed publishedAt: timestamp("published_at"), createdAt: timestamp("created_at").defaultNow(), }); // Track which users have seen which entries export const changelogViews = pgTable("changelog_views", { userId: uuid("user_id").references(() => userProfiles.id), lastSeenEntryId: uuid("last_seen_entry_id"), seenAt: timestamp("seen_at").defaultNow(), }); ``` ## Files to Create - src/app/(public)/changelog/page.tsx - src/components/changelog/whats-new-modal.tsx - src/components/changelog/update-badge.tsx - content/changelog/*.mdx - src/app/api/changelog/route.ts ## Success Criteria - Changelog updated regularly - Users notified of changes - Easy to maintain - SEO-friendly ","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-11T01:25:14.107049999-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T10:59:54.703436949-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-11T10:59:54.703436949-05:00","close_reason":"Changelog page implemented at /changelog with version history, categorized changes, timeline design, and TypeScript-native data structure."} {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-f296","title":"Implement cookie consent and GDPR banner","description":"## Origin Mirror from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-6754. Keep both repos in sync. ## Task Implement cookie consent banner for GDPR/CCPA compliance. ## Why This Matters - Legal requirement in EU (GDPR) - Legal requirement in California (CCPA) - User privacy expectations - Avoid potential fines ## Requirements ### Cookie Categories 1. **Essential** (always on) - Authentication - Security - Basic functionality 2. **Analytics** (optional) - Google Analytics - Usage tracking - Performance monitoring 3. **Marketing** (optional) - Advertising - Retargeting - Social media pixels ### Consent Banner - Shown on first visit - Clear explanation of cookies - Accept All / Customize / Reject All - Link to cookie policy - Remember preference ### Cookie Settings - Accessible from footer - Per-category toggles - Save preferences - Withdraw consent anytime ### Implementation - Don't load tracking scripts until consent - Store preference in cookie/localStorage - Respect Do Not Track header - Log consent for compliance ## Technical Implementation ```typescript // Cookie consent state interface CookieConsent { essential: true; // always true analytics: boolean; marketing: boolean; consentedAt: string; version: string; // consent policy version } ``` ### Conditional Script Loading ```tsx // Only load GA if analytics consent given {cookieConsent.analytics && (
